It implements [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) 9 using the [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) 10 library to store data, and is aimed at running production-grade CT logs. 11 12 At the moment, TesseraCT can run on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Amazon Web 13 Services (AWS), POSIX filesystems, or on vanilla 14 S3+MySQL storage systems with [different levels of maturity](#mega-status). 15 16 ## Table of contents 17 18 [:mega: Status](#mega-status) \ 19 [:motorway: Roadmap](#motorway-roadmap) \ 20 [:joystick: Usage](#joystick-usage) \ 21 [:test_tube: Public test instances](#test_tube-public-test-instances) \ 22 [:card_index_dividers: Repository structure](#card_index_dividers-repository-structure) \ 23 [:raising_hand: FAQ](#raising_hand-faq) \ 24 [:troll: History](#troll-history) \ 25 [:wrench: Contributing](#wrench-contributing) \ 26 [:page_facing_up: License](#page_facing_up-license) \ 27 [:wave: Contact](#wave-contact) 28 29 ## :mega: Status 30 31 TesseraCT is under active development. touch](#wave-contact). 81 82 ## :joystick: Usage 83 84 ### Getting Started 85 86 The most hands-on place to start is with one of the codelabs below. These 87 codelabs will guide you through bringing up your own test TesseraCT deployment: 88 89 - [GCP](./deployment/live/gcp/test) 90 - [AWS](./deployment/live/aws/test) 91 - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix#codelab) 92 - [S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/README.md#s3mysql-codelab) 93 94 We also run [public test instances](#test_tube-public-test-instances) that you 95 can interact with using [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api). 96 97 You can also have a look at the `main.go` files under [`/cmd/tesseract/`](./cmd/tesseract/) 98 to understand how to build a TesseraCT server. 99 100 Last, you can explore our [documentation](#card_index_dividers-repository-structure). 101 102 ### Running on a different platform 103 104 TesseraCT can theoretically run on any platform 105 [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) supports. 106 107 If you'd still like to run TesseraCT on a different platform that Tessera 108 supports, have a look at Tessera's [Getting Started guide](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#getting-started), 109 TesseraCT's `main.go` files under [`/cmd/tesseract/`](./cmd/tesseract/) and the 110 [architecture documentation](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/tree/main/docs/architecture.md). 111 112 We'd love to know what platform you're interested in using, 113 [come and talk to us](#wave-contact)! 114 115 ## :test_tube: Public test instances 116 117 The following logs are available for testing. Networks](https://ipng.ch/) runs [production CT logs](https://ipng.ch/s/ct/) 173 using the [POSIX implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix/). You can read more 174 about their journey, together with how they've configured TesseraCT and their 175 serving infrastructure in the great blog posts they published: 176 [1](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/07/26/certificate-transparency-part-1-tesseract/), 177 [2](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/08/10/certificate-transparency-part-2-sunlight/), 178 [3](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/08/24/certificate-transparency-part-3-operations/). 179 180 [Cheese](https://git.ipng.ch/certificate-transparency/cheese) is a tool they 181 wrote to ease deployment. 182 183 ### TrustAsia 184 185 [TrustAsia](https://www.trustasia.com/) runs [production CT logs](https://issues.chromium.org/issues/40917853#comment26) 186 using the [POSIX implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix/). 187 188 ## :card_index_dividers: Repository structure 189 190 This repository contains: 191 192 1. **[Binaries](./cmd/)**: TesseraCT and auxiliary tools 193 1. **[Deployment configs](./deployment/)**: purely informative, DO NOT 194 depend on them 195 1. **Libraries**: enabling the building of [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) 196 logs with [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera): 197 [ctlog](./ctlog.go), [storage](./storage/), ([internal](./internal/)) 198 1. Documentation 199 200 + [Configuration](/cmd/tesseract/) 201 - [GCP](/cmd/tesseract/gcp/) 202 - [AWS and S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/) 203 - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix/) 204 + [Performance](/docs/performance.md) 205 + [Architecture](/docs/architecture.md) 206 + [Deployment](/deployment/) 207 + Codelabs 208 - [GCP](/deployment/live/gcp/test/) 209 - [AWS](/deployment/live/aws/test/) 210 - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix/README.md#codelab) 211 - [S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/README.md#s3mysql-codelab) 212 + [Chain parsing with lax509](/internal/lax509/) 213 214 ## :raising_hand: FAQ 215 216 ### TesseraWhat? 217 218 TesseraCT is the concatenation of Tessera and CT (Certificate Transparency), 219 which also happens to be a [4-dimensional hypercube](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tesseract). 220 221 ### What's the difference between Tessera and TesseraCT? 222 223 [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) is a Go library for 224 building [tile-based transparency logs (tlogs)](https://c2sp.org/tlog-tiles) on 225 various deployment backends. TesseraCT is a service using the Tessera library 226 with CT specific settings to implement Certificate Transparency logs complying 227 with [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api). TesseraCT supports a 228 subset of Tessera's backends. A TesseraCT serving stack is composed of: 229 230 - one or multiple instances of a TesseraCT binary using the Tessera library, 231 providing [static-ct-api submission APIs](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#submission-apis) 232 - Tessera's backend infrastructure 233 - [static-ct-api monitoring APIs](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#monitoring-apis) 234 via direct access to Tessera's log storage system 235 - a minor additional storage system for [chain issuers](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers) 236 237 ### Why these platforms? 238 239 After chatting with various CT log operators, we decided to focus on GCP, AWS, 240 and to explore non-cloud-native deployments. We welcome feedback on these and 241 requests for additional backend implementations. If you have any, 242 [come and talk to us](#wave-contact)! 243 244 ### Which platform should I use? 245 246 ```mermaid 247 graph TD 248 A[Are you already running on:] --> B{GCP?} 249 B -- Yes --> C[Use TesseraCT for GCP] 250 B -- No --> D{AWS?} 251 D -- Yes --> E[Use TesseraCT for AWS] 252 D -- No --> F{S3 & MySQL on-prem?} 253 F -- Yes --> G[Use TesseraCT for Vanilla S3+MySQL] 254 F -- No --> H[Use TesseraCT for POSIX] 255 G -- I cannot --> I 256 H -- I cannot --> I[Get in touch] 257 ``` 258 259 ## :troll: History 260 261 TesseraCT is the successor to [Trillian's CTFE](https://github.com/google/certificate-transparency-go/tree/master/trillian/ctfe). 262 It was built upon its codebase, and introduces these main changes: 263 264 - **API**: TesseraCT implements [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) 265 rather than [RFC6962](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962). 266 - **Backend implementation**: TesseraCT uses [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) 267 rather than [Trillian](https://github.com/google/trillian). This means that 268 TesseraCT integrates entries faster, is cheaper to maintain, requires running a 269 single binary rather than 3, and does not need additional services for leader election. 270 - **Single tenancy**: One TesseraCT instance serves a single CT log, as opposed 271 to the CTFE which could serve multiple logs per instance. To run multiple logs, 272 simply bring up multiple independent TesseraCT stacks. For reliability, each log 273 can still be served by multiple TesseraCT _instances_. 274 - **Configuration**: TesseraCT is fully configured using flags, and does not 275 need a proto config anymore. 276 - **Chain parsing**: TesseraCT uses [internal/lax509](./internal/lax509/) to 277 validate certificate chains. It is built on top of Go's standard 278 [crypto/x509](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library, with a minimal set of CT 279 specific enhancements. It **does not** use the full `crypto/x509` [fork](https://github.com/google/certificate-transparency-go/tree/master/x509) 280 that the CTFE was using. This means that TesseraCT can benefit from the good care 281 and attention given to [crypto/x509](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509). **As a 282 result, a very small number of chains do not validate anymore, head over to 283 `internal/lax509`'s [README](./internal/lax509/README.md) for additional details.** 284 285 ## :wrench: Contributing 286 287 See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for details. 288 289 ## :page_facing_up: License 290 291 This repo is licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for 292 details. 293 294 ## :wave: Contact 295 296 Are you interested in running a TesseraCT instance? Do you have a feature 297 request? you can find us here: 298 299 - [GitHub issues](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues) 300 - [Slack](https://transparency-dev.slack.com/) ([invitation](https://transparency.dev/slack/)) 301 - [Mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/trillian-transparency) 302

Our primary focus so far has been on the GCP with Spanner + GCS + MIG configuration since we use it for our [public staging logs](#test_tube-public-test-instances). However, we believe all implementations are correct, and we'd love to [hear your feedback](#wave-contact) on any of these implementations. Read the FAQ to understand [why we chose these platforms](#why-these-platforms). ## :motorway: Roadmap Our objective is to allow log operators to run production static-ct-api CT logs starting with [temporal shards](https://googlechrome.github.io/CertificateTransparency/log_policy.html#temporal-sharding) covering 2026 onwards. At the moment, we are aiming for Beta in 2025Q3, and GA by the end of 2025. | # | Step | Status | Target release | | --- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :----------------: | ---------------| | 1 | Storage for GCP, AWS, and POSIX | :white_check_mark: | alpha | | 2 | Lightweight CT compatible x509 fork | :white_check_mark: | alpha | | 3 | static-ct-api APIs | :white_check_mark: | alpha | | 4 | Basic Antispam | :white_check_mark: | alpha | | 5 | Monitoring and metrics | :white_check_mark: | alpha | | 6 | Secure key management [#219](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues/219) | :hammer: | beta | | 7 | Witnessing | :white_check_mark: | beta | | 8 | Structured logging [#416](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues/416) | :hammer: | beta | | 9 | CCADB based root update [#212](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues/212) | :hammer: | beta | | 10 | Client [#664](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues/664) | :hammer: | 1.0 | | 11 | Stable APIs | :hammer: | 1.0 | Current public library APIs are unlikely to change in any significant way, however the API is subject to minor breaking changes until we tag 1.0. Any feedback is welcome. If you're interested in additional features, [get in touch](#wave-contact). ## :joystick: Usage ### Getting Started The most hands-on place to start is with one of the codelabs below. These codelabs will guide you through bringing up your own test TesseraCT deployment: - [GCP](./deployment/live/gcp/test) - [AWS](./deployment/live/aws/test) - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix#codelab) - [S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/README.md#s3mysql-codelab) We also run [public test instances](#test_tube-public-test-instances) that you can interact with using [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api). You can also have a look at the `main.go` files under [`/cmd/tesseract/`](./cmd/tesseract/) to understand how to build a TesseraCT server. Last, you can explore our [documentation](#card_index_dividers-repository-structure). ### Running on a different platform TesseraCT can theoretically run on any platform [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) supports. If you'd still like to run TesseraCT on a different platform that Tessera supports, have a look at Tessera's [Getting Started guide](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#getting-started), TesseraCT's `main.go` files under [`/cmd/tesseract/`](./cmd/tesseract/) and the [architecture documentation](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/tree/main/docs/architecture.md). We'd love to know what platform you're interested in using, [come and talk to us](#wave-contact)! ## :test_tube: Public test instances The following logs are available for testing. These logs run in a staging environment and should not used for production use cases. They accept chains rolling up to roots trusted by major root programs. These logs have been named after La Grande Arche de la Défense ([The Great Arche of the Defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grande_Arche)), a building in the outskirts of Paris, designed by [Johan Otto von Spreckelsen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Johan_Otto_von_Spreckelsen) and [Erik Reitzel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erik_Reitzel) to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the [French Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_Revolution). It is shaped like a [tesseract](#tesserawhat), and is covered with [tiled](https://c2sp.org/tlog-tiles) (transparent!) windows. ```json "description": "Google staging 'Arche2025h1'", "log_id": "v4vLUgreyaZJbsQJYM1zN+YKJbfu0ef6TGSJJcd2h2s=", "key": "MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEn7khjUQH1H3NJ/C8QmmBgzoNTptlH6hT5bgiQ6mQcYYg5KZoe4ZK4xCszXu4NH5NiLaDH0wHKsvg3RIQ+TTaag==", "submission_url": "https://arche2025h1.staging.ct.transparency.dev/", "monitoring_url": "https://storage.googleapis.com/static-ct-staging-arche2025h1-bucket/", "mmd": 60, "temporal_interval": { "start_inclusive": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z" } ``` ```json "description": "Google staging 'Arche2025h2'", "log_id": "L2UYNygi6ysgrNQ0osu5ivLTWAzifbdx/LfHcYDhOi4=", "key": "MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEGbSp66Dmq0b3QILTYVpwRgMV9v4tYG2jqBFeWUyg46yW7QL0KbSOUZjN4PYK5dPxfamSkp8Z0JEGL7IA5X9aMg==", "submission_url": "https://arche2025h2.staging.ct.transparency.dev/", "monitoring_url": "https://storage.googleapis.com/static-ct-staging-arche2025h2-bucket/", "mmd": 60, "temporal_interval": { "start_inclusive": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z" } ``` ```json "description": "Google staging 'Arche2026h1'", "log_id": "J+sqNJffaHpkC2Q4TkhW/Nyj6H+NzWbzTtbxvkKB7fw=", "key": "MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEZ+3YKoZTMruov4cmlImbk4MckBNzEdCyMuHlwGgJ8BUrzFLlR5U0619xDDXIXespkpBgCNVQAkhMTTXakM6KMg==", "submission_url": "https://arche2026h1.staging.ct.transparency.dev/", "monitoring_url": "https://storage.googleapis.com/static-ct-staging-arche2026h1-bucket/", "mmd": 60, "temporal_interval": { "start_inclusive": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-07-01T00:00:00Z" } ``` ## Other known instances ### IPng Networks [IPng Networks](https://ipng.ch/) runs [production CT logs](https://ipng.ch/s/ct/) using the [POSIX implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix/). You can read more about their journey, together with how they've configured TesseraCT and their serving infrastructure in the great blog posts they published: [1](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/07/26/certificate-transparency-part-1-tesseract/), [2](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/08/10/certificate-transparency-part-2-sunlight/), [3](https://ipng.ch/s/articles/2025/08/24/certificate-transparency-part-3-operations/). [Cheese](https://git.ipng.ch/certificate-transparency/cheese) is a tool they wrote to ease deployment. ### TrustAsia [TrustAsia](https://www.trustasia.com/) runs [production CT logs](https://issues.chromium.org/issues/40917853#comment26) using the [POSIX implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix/). ## :card_index_dividers: Repository structure This repository contains: 1. **[Binaries](./cmd/)**: TesseraCT and auxiliary tools 1. **[Deployment configs](./deployment/)**: purely informative, DO NOT depend on them 1. **Libraries**: enabling the building of [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) logs with [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera): [ctlog](./ctlog.go), [storage](./storage/), ([internal](./internal/)) 1. Documentation + [Configuration](/cmd/tesseract/) - [GCP](/cmd/tesseract/gcp/) - [AWS and S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/) - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix/) + [Performance](/docs/performance.md) + [Architecture](/docs/architecture.md) + [Deployment](/deployment/) + Codelabs - [GCP](/deployment/live/gcp/test/) - [AWS](/deployment/live/aws/test/) - [POSIX](/cmd/tesseract/posix/README.md#codelab) - [S3+MySQL](/cmd/tesseract/aws/README.md#s3mysql-codelab) + [Chain parsing with lax509](/internal/lax509/) ## :raising_hand: FAQ ### TesseraWhat? TesseraCT is the concatenation of Tessera and CT (Certificate Transparency), which also happens to be a [4-dimensional hypercube](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tesseract). ### What's the difference between Tessera and TesseraCT? [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) is a Go library for building [tile-based transparency logs (tlogs)](https://c2sp.org/tlog-tiles) on various deployment backends. TesseraCT is a service using the Tessera library with CT specific settings to implement Certificate Transparency logs complying with [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api). TesseraCT supports a subset of Tessera's backends. A TesseraCT serving stack is composed of: - one or multiple instances of a TesseraCT binary using the Tessera library, providing [static-ct-api submission APIs](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#submission-apis) - Tessera's backend infrastructure - [static-ct-api monitoring APIs](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#monitoring-apis) via direct access to Tessera's log storage system - a minor additional storage system for [chain issuers](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers) ### Why these platforms? After chatting with various CT log operators, we decided to focus on GCP, AWS, and to explore non-cloud-native deployments. We welcome feedback on these and requests for additional backend implementations. If you have any, [come and talk to us](#wave-contact)! ### Which platform should I use? ```mermaid graph TD A[Are you already running on:] --> B{GCP?} B -- Yes --> C[Use TesseraCT for GCP] B -- No --> D{AWS?} D -- Yes --> E[Use TesseraCT for AWS] D -- No --> F{S3 & MySQL on-prem?} F -- Yes --> G[Use TesseraCT for Vanilla S3+MySQL] F -- No --> H[Use TesseraCT for POSIX] G -- I cannot --> I H -- I cannot --> I[Get in touch] ``` ## :troll: History TesseraCT is the successor to [Trillian's CTFE](https://github.com/google/certificate-transparency-go/tree/master/trillian/ctfe). It was built upon its codebase, and introduces these main changes: - **API**: TesseraCT implements [static-ct-api](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) rather than [RFC6962](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962). - **Backend implementation**: TesseraCT uses [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera) rather than [Trillian](https://github.com/google/trillian). This means that TesseraCT integrates entries faster, is cheaper to maintain, requires running a single binary rather than 3, and does not need additional services for leader election. - **Single tenancy**: One TesseraCT instance serves a single CT log, as opposed to the CTFE which could serve multiple logs per instance. To run multiple logs, simply bring up multiple independent TesseraCT stacks. For reliability, each log can still be served by multiple TesseraCT _instances_. - **Configuration**: TesseraCT is fully configured using flags, and does not need a proto config anymore. - **Chain parsing**: TesseraCT uses [internal/lax509](./internal/lax509/) to validate certificate chains. It is built on top of Go's standard [crypto/x509](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library, with a minimal set of CT specific enhancements. It **does not** use the full `crypto/x509` [fork](https://github.com/google/certificate-transparency-go/tree/master/x509) that the CTFE was using. This means that TesseraCT can benefit from the good care and attention given to [crypto/x509](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509). **As a result, a very small number of chains do not validate anymore, head over to `internal/lax509`'s [README](./internal/lax509/README.md) for additional details.** ## :wrench: Contributing See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for details. ## :page_facing_up: License This repo is licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details. ## :wave: Contact Are you interested in running a TesseraCT instance? Do you have a feature request? you can find us here: - [GitHub issues](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/issues) - [Slack](https://transparency-dev.slack.com/) ([invitation](https://transparency.dev/slack/)) - [Mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/trillian-transparency)

35		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/storage"
36	)
37	
38	// ChainValidationConfig contains parameters to configure chain validation.
39	type ChainValidationConfig struct {
40		// RootsPEMFile is the path to the file containing root certificates that
41		// are acceptable to the log. The certs are served through get-roots
42		// endpoint.
43		RootsPEMFile string
44		// RootsRemoteFetchURLs configures an endpoint to fetch additional roots from.
45		RootsRemoteFetchURLs []string
46		// RootsRemoteFetchInterval configures the frequency at which to fetch
47		// roots from RootsRemoteEndpoint.
48		RootsRemoteFetchInterval time.Duration
49		// RootsRemoteFetchBackup is a persistent backup storage for roots fetched
50		// remotely.
51		RootsRemoteFetchBackup storage.RootsStorage
52		// RejectRoots is a list of hex-encoded SHA-256 fingerprints of ASN.1 DER
53		// encoded root certificates that should never be trusted.
54		RejectRoots []string
55		// RejectExpired controls if true then the certificate validity period will be
56		// checked against the current time during the validation of submissions.
57		// This will cause expired certificates to be rejected.
58		RejectExpired bool
59		// RejectUnexpired controls if TesseraCT rejects certificates that are
60		// either currently valid or not yet valid.
61		// TODO(phboneff): evaluate whether we need to keep this one.
62		RejectUnexpired bool
63		// ExtKeyUsages lists Extended Key Usage values that newly submitted
64		// certificates MUST contain. By default all are accepted. The
65		// values specified must be ones known to the x509 package, comma separated.
66		ExtKeyUsages string
67		// RejectExtensions lists X.509 extension OIDs that newly submitted
68		// certificates MUST NOT contain. Empty by default. Values must be
69		// specificed in dotted string form (e.g. "2.3.4.5").
70		RejectExtensions string
71		// NotAfterStart defines the start of the range of acceptable NotAfter
72		// values, inclusive.
73		// Leaving this unset implies no lower bound to the range.
74		NotAfterStart *time.Time
75		// NotAfterLimit defines the end of the range of acceptable NotAfter values,
76		// exclusive.
77		// Leaving this unset implies no upper bound to the range.
78		NotAfterLimit *time.Time
79		// AcceptSHA1 specifies whether cert chains using SHA-1 based signing algorithms
80		// are allowed.
81		// CAUTION: This is a temporary solution and it will eventually be removed.
82		// DO NOT depend on it.
83		AcceptSHA1 bool
84	}
85	
86	// systemTimeSource implements ct.TimeSource.
87	type systemTimeSource struct{}
88	
89	// Now returns the true current local time.
90	func (s systemTimeSource) Now() time.Time {
91		return time.Now()
92	}
93	
94	var sysTimeSource = systemTimeSource{}
95	
96	// newChainValidator checks that a chain validation config is valid,
97	// parses it, and loads resources to validate chains.
98	func newChainValidator(ctx context.Context, cfg ChainValidationConfig) (ct.ChainValidator, error) {
99		// Load the trusted roots.
100		if cfg.RootsPEMFile == "" {
101			return nil, errors.New("empty rootsPemFile")
102		}
103	
104		roots, err := x509util.NewPEMCertPool(cfg.RejectRoots)
105		if err != nil {
106			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create roots pool: %v", err)
107		}
108		if err := roots.AppendCertsFromPEMFile(cfg.RootsPEMFile); err != nil {
109			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read trusted roots from %q: %v", cfg.RootsPEMFile, err)
110		}
111	
112		if cfg.RejectExpired && cfg.RejectUnexpired {
113			return nil, errors.New("configuration would reject all certificates")
114		}
115	
116		// Validate the time interval.
117		if cfg.NotAfterStart != nil && cfg.NotAfterLimit != nil && (cfg.NotAfterLimit).Before(*cfg.NotAfterStart) {
118			return nil, fmt.Errorf("'Not After' limit %q before start %q", cfg.NotAfterLimit.Format(time.RFC3339), cfg.NotAfterStart.Format(time.RFC3339))
119		}
120	
121		var extKeyUsages []x509.ExtKeyUsage
122		// Filter which extended key usages are allowed.
123		if cfg.ExtKeyUsages != "" {
124			lExtKeyUsages := strings.Split(cfg.ExtKeyUsages, ",")
125			extKeyUsages, err = ct.ParseExtKeyUsages(lExtKeyUsages)
126			if err != nil {
127				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse ExtKeyUsages: %v", err)
128			}
129		}
130	
131		var rejectExtIds []asn1.ObjectIdentifier
132		// Filter which extensions are rejected.
133		if cfg.RejectExtensions != "" {
134			lRejectExtensions := strings.Split(cfg.RejectExtensions, ",")
135			rejectExtIds, err = ct.ParseOIDs(lRejectExtensions)
136			if err != nil {
137				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse RejectExtensions: %v", err)
138			}
139		}
140	
141		if cfg.RootsRemoteFetchBackup != nil {
142			kvs, err := cfg.RootsRemoteFetchBackup.LoadAll(ctx)
143			if err != nil {
144				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load previously remotely fetched root from remote root backup storage: %v", err)
145			}
146			certs := make([][]byte, 0, len(kvs))
147			for _, kv := range kvs {
148				certs = append(certs, kv.V)
149			}
150			parsed, added := roots.AppendCertsFromPEMs(certs...)
151			slog.InfoContext(ctx, "Fetched roots from remote root backup storage", slog.Int("fetched", len(certs)), slog.Int("parsed", parsed), slog.Int("added", added))
152		}
153	
154		if cfg.RootsRemoteFetchInterval > 0 && len(cfg.RootsRemoteFetchURLs) > 0 {
155			fetchAndAppendRemoteRoots := func(url string) {
156				rr, err := ccadb.Fetch(ctx, url, []string{ccadb.ColPEM})
157				if err != nil {
158					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "Couldn't fetch roots", slog.String("url", url), slog.Any("error", err))
159					return
160				}
161				pems := make([][]byte, 0, len(rr))
162				for _, r := range rr {
163					if len(r) < 1 {
164						slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "Couldn't parse root: empty row", slog.String("url", url))
165						continue
166					}
167					pems = append(pems, r[0])
168					if cfg.RootsRemoteFetchBackup != nil {
169						block, _ := pem.Decode(r[0])
170						if block == nil {
171							slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "Failed to decode PEM block in fetched data", slog.String("url", url))
172							continue
173						}
174						sha := sha256.Sum256(block.Bytes)
175						key := []byte(hex.EncodeToString(sha[:]))
176						if err := cfg.RootsRemoteFetchBackup.AddIfNotExist(ctx, []storage.KV{{K: key, V: r[0]}}); err != nil {
177							slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "Couldn't store roots", slog.String("key", string(key)), slog.Any("error", err))
178							continue
179						}
180					}
181				}
182				parsed, added := roots.AppendCertsFromPEMs(pems...)
183				slog.InfoContext(ctx, "Fetched roots", slog.Int("fetched", len(pems)), slog.Int("parsed", parsed), slog.Int("added", added), slog.String("url", url))
184			}
185	
186			for _, url := range cfg.RootsRemoteFetchURLs {
187				fetchAndAppendRemoteRoots(url)
188			}
189	
190			go func() {
191				ticker := time.NewTicker(cfg.RootsRemoteFetchInterval)
192				defer ticker.Stop()
193				for {
194					select {
195					case <-ctx.Done():
196						return
197					case <-ticker.C:
198						for _, url := range cfg.RootsRemoteFetchURLs {
199							fetchAndAppendRemoteRoots(url)
200						}
201					}
202				}
203			}()
204		}
205	
206		cv := ct.NewChainValidator(roots, cfg.RejectExpired, cfg.RejectUnexpired, cfg.NotAfterStart, cfg.NotAfterLimit, extKeyUsages, rejectExtIds, cfg.AcceptSHA1)
207	
208		return cv, nil
209	}
210	
211	// NotBeforeRL configures rate limits based on certificate not_before's age.
212	type NotBeforeRL struct {
213		AgeThreshold time.Duration
214		RateLimit    float64
215	}
216	
217	type LogHandlerOpts struct {
218		NotBeforeRL       *NotBeforeRL
219		DedupRL           float64
220		MaxCertChainBytes int64
221	}
222	
223	// NewLogHandler creates a Tessera based CT log plugged into HTTP handlers.
224	//
225	// HTTP server handlers implement static-ct-api submission APIs:
226	// https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api#submission-apis.
227	// It populates the data served via monitoring APIs (https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api#submission-apis)
228	// but it _does not_ implement monitoring APIs itself. Monitoring APIs should
229	// be served independently, either through the storage's system serving
230	// infrastructure directly (GCS over HTTPS for instance), or with an
231	// independent serving stack of your choice.
232	func NewLogHandler(ctx context.Context, origin string, signer crypto.Signer, cfg ChainValidationConfig, cs storage.CreateStorage, httpDeadline time.Duration, maskInternalErrors bool, pathPrefix string, opts LogHandlerOpts) (http.Handler, error) {
233		cv, err := newChainValidator(ctx, cfg)
234		if err != nil {
235			return nil, fmt.Errorf("newCertValidationOpts(): %v", err)
236		}
237		log, err := ct.NewLog(ctx, origin, signer, cv, cs, sysTimeSource)
238		if err != nil {
239			return nil, fmt.Errorf("newLog(): %v", err)
240		}
241	
242		ctOpts := &ct.HandlerOptions{
243			Deadline:           httpDeadline,
244			RequestLog:         &ct.DefaultRequestLog{},
245			MaskInternalErrors: maskInternalErrors,
246			TimeSource:         sysTimeSource,
247			PathPrefix:         pathPrefix,
248		}
249		if opts.NotBeforeRL != nil {
250			ctOpts.RateLimits.NotBefore(opts.NotBeforeRL.AgeThreshold, opts.NotBeforeRL.RateLimit)
251		}
252		if opts.DedupRL >= 0 {
253			ctOpts.RateLimits.Dedup(opts.DedupRL)
254		}
255	
256		handlers := ct.NewPathHandlers(ctx, ctOpts, log)
257		mux := http.NewServeMux()
258		// Register handlers for all the configured logs.
259		for path, handler := range handlers {
260			mux.Handle(path, http.MaxBytesHandler(handler, opts.MaxCertChainBytes))
261		}
262	
263		// Health checking endpoint.
264		mux.HandleFunc("/healthz", func(resp http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
265			_, _ = fmt.Fprint(resp, "ok")
266		})
267	
268		return mux, nil
269	}

# How to contribute

We'd love to accept your patches and contributions to this project. There are
just a few small guidelines you need to follow.

## Getting Started

If you're new to the project, a good place to start is the [main `README.md`](/README.md) file.
This will give you a high-level overview of the project and its goals.

Before you start coding, you'll need to set up your development environment.
This project uses Go, so you'll need to have a recent version of Go installed.
You can find instructions on how to do that [here](https://golang.org/doc/install).

Once you have Go installed, you can clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract.git
```

## Contributor License Agreement

Contributions to any Google project must be accompanied by a Contributor
License Agreement. This is not a copyright **assignment**, it simply gives
Google permission to use and redistribute your contributions as part of the
project.

* If you are an individual writing original source code and you're sure you
    own the intellectual property, then you'll need to sign an [individual
    CLA][].

* If you work for a company that wants to allow you to contribute your work,
    then you'll need to sign a [corporate CLA][].

You generally only need to submit a CLA once, so if you've already submitted
one (even if it was for a different project), you probably don't need to do it
again.

[individual CLA]: https://developers.google.com/open-source/cla/individual
[corporate CLA]: https://developers.google.com/open-source/cla/corporate

Once your CLA is submitted (or if you already submitted one for
another Google project), make a commit adding yourself to the
[AUTHORS][] and [CONTRIBUTORS][] files. This commit can be part
of your first [pull request][].

[AUTHORS]: AUTHORS
[CONTRIBUTORS]: CONTRIBUTORS

## Submitting a patch

  1. It's generally best to start by opening a new issue describing the bug or
     feature you're intending to fix. Even if you think it's relatively minor,
     it's helpful to know what people are working on. Mention in the initial
     issue that you are planning to work on that bug or feature so that it can
     be assigned to you.

  1. Follow the normal process of [forking][] the project, and setup a new
     branch to work in. It's important that each group of changes be done in
     separate branches in order to ensure that a pull request only includes the
     commits related to that bug or feature.

  1. Do your best to have [well-formed commit messages][] for each change.
     This provides consistency throughout the project, and ensures that commit
     messages are able to be formatted properly by various git tools.

  1. Finally, push the commits to your fork and submit a [pull request][].

## Code Style

This project follows the standard Go code style.
You can use `gofmt` to format your code before submitting a pull request.

We also use `golangci-lint` to check for common issues.
You can run it locally with:

```bash
golangci-lint run
```

[forking]: https://help.github.com/articles/fork-a-repo
[well-formed commit messages]: http://tbaggery.com/2008/04/19/a-note-about-git-commit-messages.html
[pull request]: https://help.github.com/articles/creating-a-pull-request

15	// The ct_server binary runs the CT personality.
16	package main
17	
18	import (
19		"context"
20		"crypto"
21		"crypto/x509"
22		"encoding/pem"
23		"errors"
24		"flag"
25		"fmt"
26		"log/slog"
27		"net/http"
28		_ "net/http/pprof"
29		"os"
30		"os/signal"
31		"path/filepath"
32		"strconv"
33		"strings"

38		"github.com/dgraph-io/badger/v4"
39		"github.com/dustin/go-humanize"
40		"github.com/transparency-dev/tessera"
41		tposix "github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/storage/posix"
42		tposix_as "github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/storage/posix/antispam"
43		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract"
44		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/storage"
45		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/storage/posix"
46		"golang.org/x/mod/sumdb/note"
47	
48		_ "expvar" // Registers /debug/vars, with BadgerDB metrics.
49	)
50	
51	func init() {
52		flag.Var(&notAfterStart, "not_after_start", "Start of the range of acceptable NotAfter values, inclusive. Leaving this unset implies no lower bound to the range. RFC3339 format, e.g: 2024-01-02T15:04:05Z.")
53		flag.Var(&notAfterLimit, "not_after_limit", "Cut off point of notAfter dates - only notAfter dates strictly *before* notAfterLimit will be accepted. Leaving this unset means no upper bound on the accepted range. RFC3339 format, e.g: 2024-01-02T15:04:05Z.")
54		flag.Var(&additionalSigners, "additional_signer", "Path to a file containing an additional note Signer formatted keys for checkpoints. May be specified multiple times.")
55		flag.Var(&rootsRejectFingerprints, "roots_reject_fingerprints", "Hex-encoded SHA-256 fingerprint of a root certificate to reject. May be specified multiple times.")
56		flag.Float64Var(&dedupRL, "rate_limit_dedup", 100, "Rate limit for resolving duplicate submissions, in requests per second - i.e. duplicate requests for already integrated entries, which need to be fetched from the log storage by TesseraCT to extract their timestamp. When 0, all duplicate submissions are rejected. When negative, no rate limit is applied.")
57		// DEPRECATED: will be removed shortly
58		flag.Float64Var(&dedupRL, "pushback_max_dedupe_in_flight", 100, "DEPRECATED: use rate_limit_dedup. Maximum number of in-flight duplicate add requests - i.e. the number of requests matching entries that have already been integrated, but need to be fetched by the client to retrieve their timestamp. When 0, duplicate entries are always pushed back.")
59		flag.Var(&rootsRemoteFetchURLs, "roots_remote_fetch_url", "URL to fetch additional trusted roots from. May be specified multiple times.")
60	}
61	
62	// Global flags that affect all log instances.
63	var (
64		notAfterStart           timestampFlag
65		notAfterLimit           timestampFlag
66		additionalSigners       multiStringFlag
67		rootsRejectFingerprints multiStringFlag
68		rootsRemoteFetchURLs    multiStringFlag
69		dedupRL                 float64
70	
71		// Functionality flags
72		httpEndpoint             = flag.String("http_endpoint", "localhost:6962", "Endpoint for HTTP (host:port).")
73		maxCertChainBytes        = flag.Int64("max_cert_chain_bytes", 512<<10, "Maximum size of certificate chain in bytes for add-chain and add-pre-chain endpoints (default: 512 KiB)")
74		maskInternalErrors       = flag.Bool("mask_internal_errors", false, "Don't return error strings with Internal Server Error HTTP responses.")
75		origin                   = flag.String("origin", "", "Origin of the log, for checkpoints. This MUST match the log's submission prefix as per https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api.")
76		pathPrefix               = flag.String("path_prefix", "", "Prefix to use on endpoints URL paths: HOST:PATH_PREFIX/ct/v1/ENDPOINT.")
77		rootsPemFile             = flag.String("roots_pem_file", "", "Path to the file containing root certificates that are acceptable to the log.")
78		rootsRemoteFetchInterval = flag.Duration("roots_remote_fetch_interval", time.Duration(0), "Interval between two fetches from roots_fetch_url, e.g. \"1h\". Set to \"0s\" to disable.")
79		rejectExpired            = flag.Bool("reject_expired", false, "If true then the certificate validity period will be checked against the current time during the validation of submissions. This will cause expired certificates to be rejected.")
80		rejectUnexpired          = flag.Bool("reject_unexpired", false, "If true then TesseraCT rejects certificates that are either currently valid or not yet valid.")
81		extKeyUsages             = flag.String("ext_key_usages", "", "If set, will restrict the set of such usages that the server will accept. By default only 'ServerAuth' certs are accepted. Set to 'Any' to accept all chain. Accepted values are defined in internal/ct.")
82		rejectExtensions         = flag.String("reject_extension", "", "A list of X.509 extension OIDs, in dotted string form (e.g. '2.3.4.5') which, if present, should cause submissions to be rejected.")
83		acceptSHA1               = flag.Bool("accept_sha1_signing_algorithms", true, "If true, accept chains that use SHA-1 based signing algorithms. This flag will eventually be removed, and such algorithms will be rejected.")
84		enablePublicationAwaiter = flag.Bool("enable_publication_awaiter", true, "If true, waits for the submitted certificate to be covered by a published checkpoint before responding to an add-* request.")
85		witnessPolicyFile        = flag.String("witness_policy_file", "", "(Optional) Path to the file containing the witness policy in the format described at https://git.glasklar.is/sigsum/core/sigsum-go/-/blob/main/doc/policy.md")
86		witnessTimeout           = flag.Duration("witness_timeout", tessera.DefaultWitnessTimeout, "Maximum time to wait for witness responses.")
87		notBeforeRL              = flag.String("rate_limit_old_not_before", "28h:500", "Optionally rate limits submissions with old notBefore dates. Expects a value of with the format: \"<go duration>:<rate limit>\", e.g. \"30d:50\" would impose a limit of 50 certs/s on submissions whose notBefore date is >= 30days old.")
88	
89		// Performance flags
90		httpDeadline                = flag.Duration("http_deadline", time.Second*10, "Deadline for HTTP requests.")

92		checkpointInterval          = flag.Duration("checkpoint_interval", 1500*time.Millisecond, "Interval between publishing checkpoints when the log has grown")
93		checkpointRepublishInterval = flag.Duration("checkpoint_republish_interval", 30*time.Second, "Interval between republishing a checkpoint for a log which hasn't grown since the previous checkpoint was published")
94		batchMaxSize                = flag.Uint("batch_max_size", tessera.DefaultBatchMaxSize, "Maximum number of entries to process in a single sequencing batch.")
95		batchMaxAge                 = flag.Duration("batch_max_age", tessera.DefaultBatchMaxAge, "Maximum age of entries in a single sequencing batch.")
96		pushbackMaxOutstanding      = flag.Uint("pushback_max_outstanding", tessera.DefaultPushbackMaxOutstanding, "Maximum number of in-flight add requests - i.e. the number of entries with sequence numbers assigned, but which are not yet integrated into the log.")
97		pushbackMaxAntispamLag      = flag.Uint("pushback_max_antispam_lag", tposix_as.DefaultPushbackThreshold, "Maximum permitted lag for antispam follower, before log starts returning pushback.")
98		clientHTTPTimeout           = flag.Duration("client_http_timeout", 5*time.Second, "Timeout for outgoing HTTP requests")
99		clientHTTPMaxIdle           = flag.Int("client_http_max_idle", 20, "Maximum number of idle HTTP connections for outgoing requests.")

// The ct_server binary runs the CT personality.
package main

import (
	"context"
	"crypto"
	"crypto/x509"
	"encoding/pem"
	"errors"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"

	"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/storage/posix"
	"golang.org/x/mod/sumdb/note"

	_ "expvar" // Registers /debug/vars, with BadgerDB metrics.
)

func init() {
	flag.Var(&notAfterStart, "not_after_start", "Start of the range of acceptable NotAfter values, inclusive. Leaving this unset implies no lower bound to the range. RFC3339 format, e.g: 2024-01-02T15:04:05Z.")
	flag.Var(&notAfterLimit, "not_after_limit", "Cut off point of notAfter dates - only notAfter dates strictly *before* notAfterLimit will be accepted. Leaving this unset means no upper bound on the accepted range. RFC3339 format, e.g: 2024-01-02T15:04:05Z.")
	flag.Var(&additionalSigners, "additional_signer", "Path to a file containing an additional note Signer formatted keys for checkpoints. May be specified multiple times.")
	flag.Var(&rootsRejectFingerprints, "roots_reject_fingerprints", "Hex-encoded SHA-256 fingerprint of a root certificate to reject. May be specified multiple times.")
	flag.Float64Var(&dedupRL, "rate_limit_dedup", 100, "Rate limit for resolving duplicate submissions, in requests per second - i.e. duplicate requests for already integrated entries, which need to be fetched from the log storage by TesseraCT to extract their timestamp. When 0, all duplicate submissions are rejected. When negative, no rate limit is applied.")
	// DEPRECATED: will be removed shortly
	flag.Float64Var(&dedupRL, "pushback_max_dedupe_in_flight", 100, "DEPRECATED: use rate_limit_dedup. Maximum number of in-flight duplicate add requests - i.e. the number of requests matching entries that have already been integrated, but need to be fetched by the client to retrieve their timestamp. When 0, duplicate entries are always pushed back.")
	flag.Var(&rootsRemoteFetchURLs, "roots_remote_fetch_url", "URL to fetch additional trusted roots from. May be specified multiple times.")
}

// Global flags that affect all log instances.
var (
	notAfterStart           timestampFlag
	notAfterLimit           timestampFlag
	additionalSigners       multiStringFlag
	rootsRejectFingerprints multiStringFlag
	rootsRemoteFetchURLs    multiStringFlag
	dedupRL                 float64

	// Functionality flags
	httpEndpoint             = flag.String("http_endpoint", "localhost:6962", "Endpoint for HTTP (host:port).")
	maxCertChainBytes        = flag.Int64("max_cert_chain_bytes", 512<<10, "Maximum size of certificate chain in bytes for add-chain and add-pre-chain endpoints (default: 512 KiB)")
	maskInternalErrors       = flag.Bool("mask_internal_errors", false, "Don't return error strings with Internal Server Error HTTP responses.")
	origin                   = flag.String("origin", "", "Origin of the log, for checkpoints. This MUST match the log's submission prefix as per https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api.")
	pathPrefix               = flag.String("path_prefix", "", "Prefix to use on endpoints URL paths: HOST:PATH_PREFIX/ct/v1/ENDPOINT.")
	rootsPemFile             = flag.String("roots_pem_file", "", "Path to the file containing root certificates that are acceptable to the log.")
	rootsRemoteFetchInterval = flag.Duration("roots_remote_fetch_interval", time.Duration(0), "Interval between two fetches from roots_fetch_url, e.g. \"1h\". Set to \"0s\" to disable.")
	rejectExpired            = flag.Bool("reject_expired", false, "If true then the certificate validity period will be checked against the current time during the validation of submissions. This will cause expired certificates to be rejected.")
	rejectUnexpired          = flag.Bool("reject_unexpired", false, "If true then TesseraCT rejects certificates that are either currently valid or not yet valid.")
	extKeyUsages             = flag.String("ext_key_usages", "", "If set, will restrict the set of such usages that the server will accept. By default only 'ServerAuth' certs are accepted. Set to 'Any' to accept all chain. Accepted values are defined in internal/ct.")
	rejectExtensions         = flag.String("reject_extension", "", "A list of X.509 extension OIDs, in dotted string form (e.g. '2.3.4.5') which, if present, should cause submissions to be rejected.")
	acceptSHA1               = flag.Bool("accept_sha1_signing_algorithms", true, "If true, accept chains that use SHA-1 based signing algorithms. This flag will eventually be removed, and such algorithms will be rejected.")
	enablePublicationAwaiter = flag.Bool("enable_publication_awaiter", true, "If true, waits for the submitted certificate to be covered by a published checkpoint before responding to an add-* request.")
	witnessPolicyFile        = flag.String("witness_policy_file", "", "(Optional) Path to the file containing the witness policy in the format described at https://git.glasklar.is/sigsum/core/sigsum-go/-/blob/main/doc/policy.md")
	witnessTimeout           = flag.Duration("witness_timeout", tessera.DefaultWitnessTimeout, "Maximum time to wait for witness responses.")
	notBeforeRL              = flag.String("rate_limit_old_not_before", "28h:500", "Optionally rate limits submissions with old notBefore dates. Expects a value of with the format: \"<go duration>:<rate limit>\", e.g. \"30d:50\" would impose a limit of 50 certs/s on submissions whose notBefore date is >= 30days old.")

	// Performance flags
	httpDeadline                = flag.Duration("http_deadline", time.Second*10, "Deadline for HTTP requests.")
	inMemoryAntispamCacheSize   = flag.String("inmemory_antispam_cache_size", "256k", "Maximum number of entries to keep in the in-memory antispam cache. Unitless with SI metric prefixes, such as '256k'.")
	checkpointInterval          = flag.Duration("checkpoint_interval", 1500*time.Millisecond, "Interval between publishing checkpoints when the log has grown")
	checkpointRepublishInterval = flag.Duration("checkpoint_republish_interval", 30*time.Second, "Interval between republishing a checkpoint for a log which hasn't grown since the previous checkpoint was published")
	batchMaxSize                = flag.Uint("batch_max_size", tessera.DefaultBatchMaxSize, "Maximum number of entries to process in a single sequencing batch.")
	batchMaxAge                 = flag.Duration("batch_max_age", tessera.DefaultBatchMaxAge, "Maximum age of entries in a single sequencing batch.")
	pushbackMaxOutstanding      = flag.Uint("pushback_max_outstanding", tessera.DefaultPushbackMaxOutstanding, "Maximum number of in-flight add requests - i.e. the number of entries with sequence numbers assigned, but which are not yet integrated into the log.")
	pushbackMaxAntispamLag      = flag.Uint("pushback_max_antispam_lag", tposix_as.DefaultPushbackThreshold, "Maximum permitted lag for antispam follower, before log starts returning pushback.")
	clientHTTPTimeout           = flag.Duration("client_http_timeout", 5*time.Second, "Timeout for outgoing HTTP requests")
	clientHTTPMaxIdle           = flag.Int("client_http_max_idle", 20, "Maximum number of idle HTTP connections for outgoing requests.")
	clientHTTPMaxIdlePerHost    = flag.Int("client_http_max_idle_per_host", 10, "Maximum number of idle HTTP connections per host for outgoing requests.")

1	# Architecture
2	
3	> [!NOTE]
4	> This doc is meant to be purely informative, and makes no commitment
5	> to any support level nor future plan.
6	
7	## Platforms
8	
9	At the moment, TesseraCT can run on the following platforms:
10	
11	- [Google Cloud Platform](https://cloud.google.com) (GCP)
12	- [Amazon Web Services](https://aws.amazon.com) (AWS)
13	- Vanilla S3 storage systems alongside a MySQL database
14	- POSIX filesystems
15	
16	TesseraCT is built on top of [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/).
17	At the moment, the Tessera [MySQL-only driver](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera?tab=readme-ov-file#storage-drivers)
18	is not supported by TesseraCT.
19	
20	## Common infrastructure
21	
22	Regardless of the platform it's running on, a TesseraCT instance is composed of
23	the following elements:
24	
25	1. At least one instance of a server built using the [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/)
26	library configured with the appropriate [driver](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/?tab=readme-ov-file#storage-drivers)
27	and [antispam](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/?tab=readme-ov-file#antispam)
28	implementations for that platform.
29	1. An additional storage system to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers),
30	usually co-hosted with Tessera's log storage system.
31	
32	TesseraCT uses the CT [specific](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/ct_only.go)
33	[features](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/ctonly/ct.go)
34	in Tessera to be compliant with the [static-ct-api specs](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api).
35	
36	By design, a TesseraCT server manages a single log.
37	To increase reliability, multiple identical TesseraCT instances can run
38	concurrently for a single CT log.
39	To serve multiple distinct CT logs, bring up at least one TesseraCT server per log.
40	
41	For additional details, read [Tessera's design document](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/docs/design),
42	and the platform-specific details below.
43	
44	### Antispam
45	
46	[Tessera's antispam](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/docs/design/antispam.md)
47	is used to minimise the number of duplicate entries accepted into a log.
48	
49	When Tessera detects that a submission is a duplicate of previous one, it
50	returns the index of that previous entry, without adding an additional entry.
51	This index is required, but not sufficient to build the
52	[`SignedCertificateTimestamp` (`SCT`)](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#sct-extension)
53	returned to clients. To build this `SCT`, TesseraCT also needs the timestamp of
54	that previous entry. TesseraCT fetches the previous entry at the given
55	index, and extract its timestamp to rebuild the SCT.
56	
57	TesseraCT can rate limit these duplicate submission independently from
58	non-duplicate ones, thus allowing logs to prioritize non-duplicate submissions.
59	
60	### Garbage Collection
61	
62	[Tessera's Garbage Collection] deletes partial tiles and entry bundles once a
63	corresponding full tile or entry bundle has been published. It runs
64	periodically in the background.
65	
66	### Chain filtering
67	
68	TesseraCT needs to parse incoming certificate chains to process them. This allows:
69	
70	- TesseraCT to filter and reject chains based on various criteria such as the
71	root certificates they chain up to, or their validity date
72	- TesseraCT to construct the log entries which will be added to the log
73	
74	By design, Go's [`crypto/x509`](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library blocks
75	non-standard certificate chains, such as [precertificate chains](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
76	or chains that are not safe to use.
77	TesseraCT uses [`internal/lax509`](/internal/lax509/), a lightweight fork of
78	Go's [`crypto/x509`](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library to parse chains.
79	
80	## Detailed architecture
81	
82	### Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
83	
84	This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/gcp) is composed of:
85	
86	 1. One or multiple TesseraCT servers. For reliability, multiple servers can run
87	 concurrently.
88	 1. Tessera's backend infrastructure:
89	    1. A [GCS](https://cloud.google.com/storage) bucket. TesseraCT reuses this
90	    bucket to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers).
91	    1. A [Spanner](https://cloud.google.com/spanner) database used for
92	    sequencing entries, and antispam.
93	 1. Key materials, stored in [Secret Manager](https://cloud.google.com/security/products/secret-manager)
94	
95	APIs:
96	
97	 1. Submission requests (`add-chain`, `add-pre-chain`, `get-roots`) are processed
98	 directly by TesseraCT servers.
99	 2. Monitoring requests (fetching the checkpoint, tiles, log entries and
100	 issuers) are handled directly by GCS, without going through TesseraCT
101	 servers.
102	
103	See the Tessera [GCP design doc](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/storage/gcp)
104	for additional details.
105	
106	### Amazon Web Services (AWS)
107	
108	This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/aws) is composed of:
109	
110	 1. One or multiple TesseraCT servers. For reliability, multiple servers can run
111	 concurrently.
112	 1. Tessera's backend infrastructure:
113	    1. An [S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) bucket. TesseraCT reuses this bucket
114	    to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers).
115	    1. A MySQL [RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) database used for sequencing
116	    entries, and antispam.
117	 1. Key materials, stored in [Secrets Manager](https://aws.amazon.com/secrets-manager/)
118	
119	APIs:
120	
121	 1. Submission requests (`add-chain`, `add-pre-chain`, `get-roots`) are processed
122	directly by TesseraCT servers.
123	 2. Monitoring requests (fetching the checkpoint, tiles, log entries and
124	issuers) are handled directly by S3, without going through TesseraCT
125	servers.
126	
127	See the Tessera [AWS design doc](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/storage/aws)
128	for additional details.
129	
130	### Vanilla S3+MySQL
131	
132	While TesseraCT's [AWS implementation](#amazon-web-services-aws)
133	uses [RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) and [S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/),
134	its configuration APIs are intended to be compatible with any MySQL database,
135	and S3 compatible backend, such as [MinIO](https://min.io). If you are already
136	running these services, this might be a good option to consider depending on
137	your needs.
138	
139	> [!WARNING]
140	> S3-compatible backends do not all provide the same guarantees
141	> that S3 does, and might therefore not be suitable to run TesseraCT.
142	
143	### POSIX filesystems
144	
145	This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix) needs only:
146	
147	1. A POSIX-compliant filesystem (e.g. ZFS) to store the log, and
148	1. Any HTTP server capable of directly serving the files from the log stored on
149	that filesystem.
150	
151	See the Tessera [POSIX design doc](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/storage/posix)
152	for additional details.
153	
154	If you are comfortable running a web-server and unix-style binaries in a VM
155	(or on bare metal) and do not want the complexity of running additional storage
156	and database services, this might be a good option.
157

# Architecture

> [!NOTE]
> This doc is meant to be purely informative, and makes no commitment
> to any support level nor future plan.

## Platforms

At the moment, TesseraCT can run on the following platforms:

- [Google Cloud Platform](https://cloud.google.com) (GCP)
- [Amazon Web Services](https://aws.amazon.com) (AWS)
- Vanilla S3 storage systems alongside a MySQL database
- POSIX filesystems

TesseraCT is built on top of [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/).
At the moment, the Tessera [MySQL-only driver](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera?tab=readme-ov-file#storage-drivers)
is not supported by TesseraCT.

## Common infrastructure

Regardless of the platform it's running on, a TesseraCT instance is composed of
the following elements:

1. At least one instance of a server built using the [Tessera](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/)
library configured with the appropriate [driver](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/?tab=readme-ov-file#storage-drivers)
and [antispam](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/?tab=readme-ov-file#antispam)
implementations for that platform.
1. An additional storage system to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers),
usually co-hosted with Tessera's log storage system.

TesseraCT uses the CT [specific](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/ct_only.go)
[features](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/ctonly/ct.go)
in Tessera to be compliant with the [static-ct-api specs](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api).

By design, a TesseraCT server manages a single log.
To increase reliability, multiple identical TesseraCT instances can run
concurrently for a single CT log.
To serve multiple distinct CT logs, bring up at least one TesseraCT server per log.

For additional details, read [Tessera's design document](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/docs/design),
and the platform-specific details below.

### Antispam

[Tessera's antispam](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/blob/main/docs/design/antispam.md)
is used to minimise the number of duplicate entries accepted into a log.

When Tessera detects that a submission is a duplicate of previous one, it
returns the index of that previous entry, without adding an additional entry.
This index is required, but not sufficient to build the
[`SignedCertificateTimestamp` (`SCT`)](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#sct-extension)
returned to clients. To build this `SCT`, TesseraCT also needs the timestamp of
that previous entry. TesseraCT fetches the previous entry at the given
index, and extract its timestamp to rebuild the SCT.

TesseraCT can rate limit these duplicate submission independently from
non-duplicate ones, thus allowing logs to prioritize non-duplicate submissions.

### Garbage Collection

[Tessera's Garbage Collection] deletes partial tiles and entry bundles once a
corresponding full tile or entry bundle has been published. It runs
periodically in the background.

### Chain filtering

TesseraCT needs to parse incoming certificate chains to process them. This allows:

- TesseraCT to filter and reject chains based on various criteria such as the
root certificates they chain up to, or their validity date
- TesseraCT to construct the log entries which will be added to the log

By design, Go's [`crypto/x509`](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library blocks
non-standard certificate chains, such as [precertificate chains](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
or chains that are not safe to use.
TesseraCT uses [`internal/lax509`](/internal/lax509/), a lightweight fork of
Go's [`crypto/x509`](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509) library to parse chains.

## Detailed architecture

### Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/gcp) is composed of:

 1. One or multiple TesseraCT servers. For reliability, multiple servers can run
 concurrently.
 1. Tessera's backend infrastructure:
    1. A [GCS](https://cloud.google.com/storage) bucket. TesseraCT reuses this
    bucket to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers).
    1. A [Spanner](https://cloud.google.com/spanner) database used for
    sequencing entries, and antispam.
 1. Key materials, stored in [Secret Manager](https://cloud.google.com/security/products/secret-manager)

APIs:

 1. Submission requests (`add-chain`, `add-pre-chain`, `get-roots`) are processed
 directly by TesseraCT servers.
 2. Monitoring requests (fetching the checkpoint, tiles, log entries and
 issuers) are handled directly by GCS, without going through TesseraCT
 servers.

See the Tessera [GCP design doc](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/storage/gcp)
for additional details.

### Amazon Web Services (AWS)

This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/aws) is composed of:

 1. One or multiple TesseraCT servers. For reliability, multiple servers can run
 concurrently.
 1. Tessera's backend infrastructure:
    1. An [S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) bucket. TesseraCT reuses this bucket
    to store [issuer certificates](https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/static-ct-api.md#issuers).
    1. A MySQL [RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) database used for sequencing
    entries, and antispam.
 1. Key materials, stored in [Secrets Manager](https://aws.amazon.com/secrets-manager/)

APIs:

 1. Submission requests (`add-chain`, `add-pre-chain`, `get-roots`) are processed
directly by TesseraCT servers.
 2. Monitoring requests (fetching the checkpoint, tiles, log entries and
issuers) are handled directly by S3, without going through TesseraCT
servers.

See the Tessera [AWS design doc](https://github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/tree/main/storage/aws)
for additional details.

### Vanilla S3+MySQL

While TesseraCT's [AWS implementation](#amazon-web-services-aws)
uses [RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) and [S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/),
its configuration APIs are intended to be compatible with any MySQL database,
and S3 compatible backend, such as [MinIO](https://min.io). If you are already
running these services, this might be a good option to consider depending on
your needs.

> [!WARNING]
> S3-compatible backends do not all provide the same guarantees
> that S3 does, and might therefore not be suitable to run TesseraCT.

### POSIX filesystems

This [implementation](/cmd/tesseract/posix) needs only:

1. A POSIX-compliant filesystem (e.g. ZFS) to store the log, and
1. Any HTTP server capable of directly serving the files from the log stored on
that filesystem.

See the Tessera [POSIX design doc](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/storage/posix)
for additional details.

If you are comfortable running a web-server and unix-style binaries in a VM
(or on bare metal) and do not want the complexity of running additional storage
and database services, this might be a good option.

15	package storage
16	
17	import (
18		"bytes"
19		"context"
20		"crypto/sha256"
21		"crypto/x509"
22		"encoding/hex"
23		"errors"
24		"fmt"
25		"log/slog"
26		"os"
27		"strconv"
28		"sync"
29		"time"
30	
31		"github.com/transparency-dev/tessera"
32		"github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/api/layout"
33		"github.com/transparency-dev/tessera/ctonly"
34		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/internal/logger"
35		"github.com/transparency-dev/tesseract/internal/types/staticct"
36	
37		"golang.org/x/mod/sumdb/note"
38	)
39	
40	// CreateStorage instantiates a Tessera storage implementation with a signer option.
41	type CreateStorage func(context.Context, note.Signer) (*CTStorage, error)
42	
43	const (
44		// Each key is 64 bytes long, so this will take up to 64MB.
45		// A CT log references ~15k unique issuer certifiates in 2024, so this gives plenty of space
46		// if we ever run into this limit, we should re-think how it works.
47		maxCachedIssuerKeys        = 1 << 20
48		RootsPrefix                = "roots/"
49		DefaultAwaiterPollInterval = 200 * time.Millisecond
50	)
51	
52	type KV struct {
53		K []byte
54		V []byte
55	}
56	
57	// IssuerStorage issuer certificates under their hex encoded sha256.
58	type IssuerStorage interface {
59		AddIfNotExist(ctx context.Context, kv []KV) error
60	}
61	
62	// RootsStorage stores root certificates under their hex encoded sha256.
63	type RootsStorage interface {
64		AddIfNotExist(ctx context.Context, kv []KV) error
65		LoadAll(ctx context.Context) ([]KV, error)
66	}
67	
68	type CTStorageOptions struct {
69		Appender            *tessera.Appender
70		Reader              tessera.LogReader
71		IssuerStorage       IssuerStorage
72		AwaiterPollInterval time.Duration
73		EnablePubAwaiter    bool
74	}
75	
76	// CTStorage implements ct.Storage and tessera.LogReader.
77	type CTStorage struct {
78		storeData        func(context.Context, *ctonly.Entry) tessera.IndexFuture
79		storeIssuers     func(context.Context, []KV) error
80		reader           tessera.LogReader
81		awaiter          *tessera.PublicationAwaiter
82		enablePubAwaiter bool
83	}
84	
85	// NewCTStorage instantiates a CTStorage object.
86	func NewCTStorage(ctx context.Context, opts *CTStorageOptions) (*CTStorage, error) {
87		pollInterval := opts.AwaiterPollInterval
88		if pollInterval <= 0 {
89			pollInterval = DefaultAwaiterPollInterval
90		}
91		awaiter := tessera.NewPublicationAwaiter(ctx, opts.Reader.ReadCheckpoint, pollInterval)
92		ctStorage := &CTStorage{
93			storeData:        tessera.NewCertificateTransparencyAppender(opts.Appender),
94			storeIssuers:     cachedStoreIssuers(opts.IssuerStorage),
95			reader:           opts.Reader,
96			awaiter:          awaiter,
97			enablePubAwaiter: opts.EnablePubAwaiter,
98		}
99	
100		return ctStorage, nil
101	}
102	
103	// DedupFuture returns the timestamp matching a future.
104	//
105	// It waits for the entry matching the future to be integrated, fetches it and
106	// extracts the timestamp from it.
107	//
108	// TODO(phbnf): cache timestamps (or more) to avoid reparsing the entire leaf bundle
109	func (cts *CTStorage) DedupFuture(ctx context.Context, f tessera.IndexFuture) (uint64, error) {
110		return trace1(ctx, "tesseract.storage.DedupFuture", func(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error) {
111			idx, cpRaw, err := cts.awaiter.Await(ctx, f)
112			if err != nil {
113				return 0, fmt.Errorf("error waiting for Tessera index future and its integration: %w", err)
114			}
115	
116			// A https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api logsize is on the second line
117			l := bytes.SplitN(cpRaw, []byte("\n"), 3)
118			if len(l) < 2 {
119				return 0, errors.New("invalid checkpoint - no size")
120			}
121			ckptSize, err := strconv.ParseUint(string(l[1]), 10, 64)
122			if err != nil {
123				return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid checkpoint - can't extract size: %v", err)
124			}
125	
126			eBIdx := idx.Index / layout.EntryBundleWidth
127			eBRaw, err := cts.reader.ReadEntryBundle(ctx, eBIdx, layout.PartialTileSize(0, eBIdx, ckptSize))
128			if err != nil {
129				if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
130					return 0, fmt.Errorf("leaf bundle at index %d not found: %v", eBIdx, err)
131				}
132				return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch entry bundle at index %d: %v", eBIdx, err)
133			}
134			eIdx := idx.Index % layout.EntryBundleWidth
135			t, err := timestamp(eBRaw, eIdx)
136			if err != nil {
137				return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to extract timestamp of entry %d in bundle index %d: %v", eIdx, eBIdx, err)
138			}
139			return t, nil
140		})
141	}
142	
143	// Add stores CT entries.
144	func (cts *CTStorage) Add(ctx context.Context, entry *ctonly.Entry) (tessera.IndexFuture, error) {
145		return trace1(ctx, "tesseract.storage.Add", func(ctx context.Context) (tessera.IndexFuture, error) {
146			future := cts.storeData(ctx, entry)
147	
148			if cts.enablePubAwaiter {
149				_, _, err := cts.awaiter.Await(ctx, future)
150				if err != nil {
151					return future, fmt.Errorf("error waiting for Tessera index future and its integration: %w", err)
152				}
153			}
154	
155			return future, nil
156		})
157	}
158	
159	// AddIssuerChain stores every chain certificate under its sha256.
160	//
161	// If an object is already stored under this hash, continues.
162	func (cts *CTStorage) AddIssuerChain(ctx context.Context, chain []*x509.Certificate) error {
163		return traceErr(ctx, "tesseract.storage.AddIssuerChain", func(ctx context.Context) error {
164	
165			kvs := []KV{}
166			for _, c := range chain {
167				id := sha256.Sum256(c.Raw)
168				key := []byte(hex.EncodeToString(id[:]))
169				kvs = append(kvs, KV{K: key, V: c.Raw})
170			}
171			if err := cts.storeIssuers(ctx, kvs); err != nil {
172				return fmt.Errorf("error storing intermediates: %v", err)
173			}
174			return nil
175		})
176	}
177	
178	// cachedStoreIssuers returns a caching wrapper for an IssuerStorage
179	//
180	// This is intended to make querying faster. It does not keep a copy of the certs, only sha256.
181	// Only up to maxCachedIssuerKeys keys will be stored locally.
182	func cachedStoreIssuers(s IssuerStorage) func(context.Context, []KV) error {
183		var mu sync.RWMutex
184		m := make(map[string]struct{})
185		return func(ctx context.Context, kv []KV) error {
186			req := []KV{}
187			for _, kv := range kv {
188				mu.RLock()
189				_, ok := m[string(kv.K)]
190				mu.RUnlock()
191				if ok {
192					logger.DebugExtraContext(ctx, "cachedStoreIssuers wrapper: found in local key cache", slog.String("key", string(kv.K)))
193					continue
194				}
195				req = append(req, kv)
196			}
197			if err := s.AddIfNotExist(ctx, req); err != nil {
198				return fmt.Errorf("issuerStorage.AddIfNotExist(): error storing issuer data in the underlying IssuerStorage: %v", err)
199			}
200			for _, kv := range req {
201				if len(m) >= maxCachedIssuerKeys {
202					logger.DebugExtraContext(ctx, "cachedStoreIssuers wrapper: local issuer cache full, will stop caching issuers.")
203					return nil
204				}
205				mu.Lock()
206				m[string(kv.K)] = struct{}{}
207				mu.Unlock()
208			}
209			return nil
210		}
211	}
212	
213	// timestamp extracts the timestamp from the Nth entry in the provided serialised entry bundle.
214	//
215	// This implementation attempts to avoid any unecessary allocation or parsing other than whatever is
216	// necessary to skip over uninteresting bytes to find the requested timestamp.
217	func timestamp(ebRaw []byte, N uint64) (uint64, error) {
218		return staticct.ExtractTimestampFromBundle(ebRaw, N)
219	}

10	> unless required by a security vulnerability or a critical functionality issue.
11	
12	## To be or not to be
13	
14	As specified by [RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
15	CT logs MUST validate submitted chains to ensure that they link up to roots
16	they accept. `crypto/x509` implements this, and also runs additional common
17	chain validation checks. However, these additional checks:
18	
19	- Do not allow chains to contain precertificate or preissuer intermediates.
20	- Would block non-compliant certificates signed by production roots from being
21	accepted, thereby preventing them from becoming discoverable.
22	
23	## The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune
24	
25	The fork in this directory implements chain verification requirements from
26	[RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1) and disables
27	some additional checks, such as:
28	
29	- **Handling of critical extensions**: CT precertificates are identified by a
30	critical extension defined in [RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
31	 which the `crypto/x509` library, by design, does not process. A non-processed
32	 critical extension would fail certificate validation. This check is disabled to
33	 allow precertificate in the logs.
34	- **Cert expiry**: `notBefore` and `notAfter` certificate checks are handled at
35	submission time, based on the `notBeforeLimit` and `notAfterLimit` log
36	parameters. Therefore, not only we do not need to check them again at
37	certificate verification time, but we specifically want to accept all
38	certificates within the `[notBeforeLimit, notAfterLimit]` range, even if they

## To be or not to be

As specified by [RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
CT logs MUST validate submitted chains to ensure that they link up to roots
they accept. `crypto/x509` implements this, and also runs additional common
chain validation checks. However, these additional checks:

- Do not allow chains to contain precertificate or preissuer intermediates.
- Would block non-compliant certificates signed by production roots from being
accepted, thereby preventing them from becoming discoverable.

## The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune

The fork in this directory implements chain verification requirements from
[RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1) and disables
some additional checks, such as:

- **Handling of critical extensions**: CT precertificates are identified by a
critical extension defined in [RFC6962 §3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.1),
 which the `crypto/x509` library, by design, does not process. A non-processed
 critical extension would fail certificate validation. This check is disabled to
 allow precertificate in the logs.
- **Cert expiry**: `notBefore` and `notAfter` certificate checks are handled at
submission time, based on the `notBeforeLimit` and `notAfterLimit` log
parameters. Therefore, not only we do not need to check them again at
certificate verification time, but we specifically want to accept all
certificates within the `[notBeforeLimit, notAfterLimit]` range, even if they
have expired.
- **CA name restrictions**: an intermediate or root certificate can restrict the

2	
3	This hammer sets up read and write traffic to a Static CT API log to test
4	correctness and performance under load.  The traffic is sent according to the
5	[The Static Certificate Transparency API](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) spec.
6	
7	## UI
8	
9	The hammer runs using a text-based UI in the terminal that shows the current
10	status, logs, and supports increasing/decreasing read and write traffic.
11	The process can be killed with `<Ctrl-C>`.
12	This TUI allows for a level of interactivity when probing a new configuration of
13	a log in order to find any cliffs where performance degrades.
14	
15	For real load-testing applications, especially headless runs as part of a CI
16	pipeline, it is recommended to run the tool with `show_ui=false` in order to
17	disable the UI.
18	
19	## Usage

# Hammer: A load testing tool for Static CT API logs

This hammer sets up read and write traffic to a Static CT API log to test
correctness and performance under load.  The traffic is sent according to the
[The Static Certificate Transparency API](https://c2sp.org/static-ct-api) spec.

## UI

The hammer runs using a text-based UI in the terminal that shows the current
status, logs, and supports increasing/decreasing read and write traffic.
The process can be killed with `<Ctrl-C>`.
This TUI allows for a level of interactivity when probing a new configuration of
a log in order to find any cliffs where performance degrades.

For real load-testing applications, especially headless runs as part of a CI
pipeline, it is recommended to run the tool with `show_ui=false` in order to
disable the UI.

## Usage
